title: Propose dispatcher fix: operator-explicit HNOTEs in monitor/analyst/human-notes.json should ride above Mode 1 (deep-attack singleton) in analyst's priority order. Current placement (Mode 2, after Mode 1) means a Mode 1 backlog of 20+ items blocks HNOTE consumption for ~a day, which is too slow for operator commissions.
scope_authority: Design proposal only (PROP-NNN). Operator reviews proposal before any prompt edits land. Tinker investigates, proposes the canonical fix, does NOT self-apply. analyst.md is load-bearing — small dispatcher changes can have cascade effects on the routing flow.
context: Observed 2026-05-16 ~12:00-13:00 UTC: I filed HNOTE-OPERATOR-WIN-012-COMPRESSION-EXPERIMENT-001 commissioning a content-compression experiment. The HNOTE is exactly the kind of operator commission that motivated Mode 2 existing.
Analyst's 11:06 UTC run executed Mode 1 (deep-attack singleton on EXP-398, closing ISS-1191). It did NOT consume the HNOTE.

Analyst's queue at that time: pending-tracker=21 (Mode 1 work), pending-inbox=16. Mode 1 has higher priority than Mode 2 in the dispatcher, so 21 Mode 1 items must clear before any Mode 2 HNOTE picks up. At ~1 deep-attack per run hourly, that's ~21 hours.

Operator chose option-1 ('cowork-claude does the compression in-session') rather than wait. The experiment was successful (EXP-401, 1888w → 528w, integrated commit 66c9a74) but the underlying dispatcher problem remains.
The dispatcher priority order in monitor/prompts/analyst.md is documented as:

The problem: Mode 1's tracker queue is currently 20+ items deep and the routine drain rate is slow. Operator HNOTEs are 'I want this specific thing done' commissions — they should not sit behind 'work through the BAU queue first'.

Mode 5 (Frozen Prediction Drafts) was created earlier as an operator-explicit channel above the rest, but Mode 5 is at priority 8 (below Mode 4!) in the documented dispatcher. Its actual priority must be operator-explicit invocation, not dispatcher order. So Mode 5 isn't actually a 'rides above Mode 1' channel — it's a 'never run on cadence, only when explicitly commissioned' channel.
What's needed: a way for the operator to mark an HNOTE as 'commission — runs before BAU work, like Mode 0/Mode 5 priority'. Options below.

Related fix that compounds this: the existing Mode 5 has a 'frozen prediction' detector (looks for 'mode: 5' or 'frozen prediction' in the body). The new fix should use a similar trigger — e.g., 'priority: commission' or 'mode: commission' or 'target_agents includes analyst AND priority=high'.
operator_intent: Tinker proposes a small, focused PROP that adds an 'operator commission' priority tier to the analyst dispatcher. Tier should sit above Mode 1 but below Mode 0 (Mode 0 is WIN-count parity, which is always top). The trigger should be explicit and operator-controlled, so routine HNOTEs (e.g., 'while you're in there, also consider X') don't accidentally jump the queue and block Mode 1 drainage indefinitely.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Re-read monitor/prompts/analyst.md Dispatcher section in full. Confirm Mode order. Note where Mode 5's 'never on cadence' trigger sits — that pattern is the closest precedent for what this directive wants.
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: Short summary of current dispatcher state + Mode 5 trigger pattern.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Propose the canonical fix. Options to evaluate:

options.option_A_new_mode_zero_point_five: Insert a new 'Mode 0.5 — Operator Commission' between Mode 0 (WIN onboarding) and Mode 1. Trigger: HNOTE has `priority: 'commission'` OR `target_agents` includes 'analyst' AND `priority: 'high'`. Process the matching HNOTE before any Mode 1/2/2b/3/4 work. Most operator-friendly framing.
options.option_B_priority_field_promotion: Keep Mode 2 in its current position, but add a 'priority promotion' check at the top of the dispatcher: 'before evaluating Modes 0-5, scan human-notes.json for any pending note with priority:commission or priority:high+commission-flagged — if found, process THAT note first as an inline Mode 2 override'. Less structural change, slightly less elegant in the prompt.
options.option_C_mode_5_generalization: Generalize Mode 5 from 'frozen prediction drafts only' to 'any operator-explicit HNOTE commission'. Rename to 'Mode 5 — Operator Commission Drafts' (or just 'Mode 5 — Operator Commissions'). Move Mode 5 to the position just below Mode 0. Keep the 'never on cadence; only when explicitly commissioned' trigger but expand the body-detection regex to recognize a broader set of commission types (frozen-prediction, compression, custom-WIN-rewrite, etc.). Most consistent with existing pattern.
options.option_D_dispatcher_inversion: Invert the current Mode 1 / Mode 2 priority: HNOTEs always come first; Mode 1 (deep-attack) runs only after Mode 2 is drained. Aggressive change — operator HNOTEs would always block deep-attack work. Probably too far in the other direction.

design_questions_for_tinker: Q1: Which option (A/B/C/D)? Operator-stated preference: explicit mechanism + minimal-change-to-prompt + consistent with existing Mode 5 pattern. Tinker recommends one with rationale.

Q2: What's the exact field/flag the operator sets in the HNOTE to invoke commission priority? `priority: 'commission'`? Or `priority: 'high'` AND `target_agents` exact-match analyst? Or a new field `commission: true`? Tinker recommends one with rationale.

Q3: What's the guard against abuse? If every HNOTE the operator files becomes 'commission', the routine work never drains. Recommend: documentation in the operator-facing parts of CLAUDE.md or analyst.md that says 'commission priority is reserved for operator-explicit time-sensitive commissions; routine guidance HNOTEs should use priority:high or :medium without commission flag'. Tinker propose the discipline.

Q4: Backward-compat: existing HNOTEs in human-notes.json don't have the new field. The dispatcher check should default to the current behavior (Mode 2 ordering) when the flag is absent. Tinker confirm.

Q5: Cross-agent: does curmudgeon-side or decider-side HNOTE consumption have the same problem (HNOTEs sit behind routine work)? If yes, the fix should apply symmetrically. If no, this PROP is analyst-scope only. Tinker check the other two agents' prompts and report.
tasks_for_tinker.deliverable: Recommendation with rationale, including answers to Q1-Q5.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Steelman the case for NOT making this change. Strongest counters:
counter_args_to_address: 'Operator should just wait. The Mode 1 queue is real work; the operator's commission is one of many priorities competing for analyst-Opus tokens.' Rebuttal expected: routine work has a recurring cadence and self-balances; one-off operator commissions don't fit that model.

'If commission priority exists, every routine HNOTE will be flagged commission and routine work never drains.' Rebuttal expected: discipline rule + audit (Q3). Operator agreed to be disciplined; if telemetry shows abuse, tighten the trigger.

'The right fix is increasing analyst cadence, not changing priority. Hourly analyst runs already process a lot — bump to every 30 minutes and the queue drains faster.' Rebuttal expected: cost. Analyst is Opus. Doubling cadence doubles cost without proportional benefit. Priority reordering is free.

tasks_for_tinker.description: Author the PROP as DESIGN-PENDING-OPERATOR-REVIEW. Do NOT self-apply. Operator reviews proposal, requests amendments if needed, then operator + cowork-claude apply the analyst.md edits.

in_scope_for_tinker: Read monitor/prompts/analyst.md Dispatcher section in full
Read Mode 5 trigger pattern for precedent
Check curmudgeon.md and decider-intake.md for analogous HNOTE-consumption ordering (Q5)
Author one PROP with the full design
out_of_scope_for_tinker: Do NOT modify analyst.md or any prompt directly. Propose-only.
Do NOT propose changes to curmudgeon-side or decider-side HNOTE consumption unless Q5 surfaces the same problem there — if it does, propose the cross-agent fix; if not, scope this PROP to analyst.

Do NOT batch with DIRECTIVE-005 (Categories F/G/H). Each gets its own PROP. The compression-experiment finding I added as an addendum to DIRECTIVE-005 already references this directive — but they're distinct PROPs.
operator_preferences_for_proposal: Counter-argument section mandatory (per operator preference 'Think about the other side of the argument too')
Recommend the option that minimizes prompt-change risk and is most consistent with existing dispatcher patterns
Discipline rule for commission-priority abuse (Q3) is essential — without it, the fix degrades into 'everything is commission priority'
Cross-agent check (Q5) is critical — if the same problem exists at curmudgeon or decider, fix it symmetrically
